to create .html documentation from the JavaScript .js files. JavaScript Accessors Documentation JSDoc output, updated every 5 minutes JavaScript Accessors Documentation (JSDoc) <--- Info about accessors JSDoc output for ptII modules (ptII repo) See

sors JSDoc output for ptII modules (ptII repo) See Also JSDoc - overview of JSDoc Software Configuration Management Tags See Version 0 - 1a AccessorSpecification Documentation for a description of the tags @accessor @input @output @parameter JSDoc Cu

tags @accessor @input @output @parameter JSDoc Custom Tag Plugin - How the above tags are supported Run JSDoc To run JSDoc in your local tree, cd to accessors/web and run ant . The ant command will download jsdoc using npm install @terraswarm/jsdoc

download jsdoc using npm install @terraswarm/jsdoc . The output will appear in accessors/web/doc/jsdoc/index.html JSDoc Formatting See http://usejsdoc.org/ for documentation about tags JSDoc Arrays Arrays should be specified as: * @output {array<{'ho

rays should be specified as: * @output {array<{'horizontal': 'number', 'vertical': 'number'}>} cog The horizontal and vertical position of the center of gravity of motion, in pixels. Not * @output {[number, number]} cog The horizontal and vertical po

umber, number]} cog The horizontal and vertical position of the center of gravity of motion, in pixels. Otherwise you will get [exec] ERROR: Unable to parse a tag's type expression for source file /Users/cxh/ptII/\ org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/w

ers/cxh/ptII/\ org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/image/MotionDetector.js with tag title "output" and t\ ext "{[number, number]} cog The horizontal and vertical position of the center of gravity o\ f motion, in pixels.": Invalid type expression "[

Configuration accessors/web/jsdoc/jsdoc.json is used to configure JSDoc. Configuring JSDoc with conf.json - Formatting for the .json file (usejsdoc.org) Below are the contents of jsdoc.json ''Note that the version in the svn repo might be different {

t the version in the svn repo might be different { "plugins" : [ "plugins/markdown" , "jsdoc/plugins/accessorJSDocTags" ] , "markdown" : { "tags" : [ "accessor" , "input" , "output" , "parameter" ] } , "source" : { "excludePattern" : "(doc|browser/co

, "source" : { "excludePattern" : "(doc|browser/common|browser/test/lib|node_modules|vendors)" } } [$[Get Code]] plugins Causes jsdoc to use read accessors/web/jsdoc/accessorJSDocTags.js . This is use by the ptdoc template jsdoc was failing with Com

mozilla . javascript . optimizer . OptRuntime . callProp0 ( OptRuntime. java : 85 ) [$[Get Code]] It looks like the problem is trying to run jsdoc on mocha.js. The fix is to exclude browser/test/lib from jsdoc by adding that path to jsdoc.js. This

from jsdoc by adding that path to jsdoc.js. This problem does not occur in the default JSDoc that does not include our accessor hacks. PtDoc In Vergil, when an accessor is loaded or reloaded, a doc file is also downloaded and the contents of the doc

ile is also downloaded and the contents of the doc file are merged in to the model. The doc file ends in PtDoc.xml . For example, the accessor https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/cameras/Camera.js has a corresponding PtDoc file: https://ptolemy.be

has a corresponding PtDoc file: https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/cameras/CameraPtDoc.xml The *.js files are read in and we use a plugin and a template to create *PtDoc.xml output. To run this: cd accessors / web ant ptdoc [$[Get Code]] What hap

d accessors / web ant ptdoc [$[Get Code]] What happens is that ant runs jsdoc with the following command line options: --configure jsdoc/jsdoc.json Use the configuration file from accessors/web/jsdoc/jsdoc.json which configures JSDoc to use the plugi

jsdoc.json which configures JSDoc to use the plugin at accessors/web/jsdoc/plugins/accessorJSDocTags.js . --template jsdoc/templates/ptdoc Use the template in accessors/web/jsdoc/templates/ptdoc/publish.js . PtDoc Plugin accessors/web/jsdoc/plugins/a

sh.js . PtDoc Plugin accessors/web/jsdoc/plugins/accessorJSDocTags.js looks like: Note that this may have changed, see https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/jsdoc/plugins/accessorJSDocTags.js for an uptodate version'' exports. defineTags = function

uptodate version'' exports. defineTags = function ( dictionary ) { dictionary. defineTag ( "accessor" , { mustHaveValue : true , onTagged : function ( doclet , tag ) { doclet. accessor = tag. name ; } } ) ; dictionary. defineTag ( "input" , { mustHav

} ) ; dictionary. defineTag ( "input" , { mustHaveValue : true , canHaveType : true , canHaveName : true , onTagged : function ( doclet , tag ) { if ( ! doclet. inputs ) { doclet. inputs = [ ] ; } doclet. inputs . push ( tag. value ) ; } } ) ; dicti

clet. inputs . push ( tag. value ) ; } } ) ; dictionary. defineTag ( "output" , { mustHaveValue : true , canHaveType : true , canHaveName : true , onTagged : function ( doclet , tag ) { if ( ! doclet. outputs ) { doclet. outputs = [ ] ; } doclet. out

outputs ) { doclet. outputs = [ ] ; } doclet. outputs . push ( tag. value ) ; } } ) ; dictionary. defineTag ( "parameter" , { mustHaveValue : true , canHaveType : true , canHaveName : true , onTagged : function ( doclet , tag ) { if ( ! doclet. para

: function ( doclet , tag ) { if ( ! doclet. parameters ) { doclet. parameters = [ ] ; } doclet. parameters . push ( tag. value ) ; } } ) ; } ; [$[Get Code]] What the above code does is push values for the @accessor etc. tags into JavaScript arrays.

or the @accessor etc. tags into JavaScript arrays. For notes, see: https://chess.eecs.berkeley.edu/ptexternal/wiki/Main/JSDoc#JSDocCustomTags PtDoc Template The template file at accessors/web/jsdoc/templates/ptdoc/publish.js (See https://ptolemy.berk

mplates/ptdoc/publish.js (See https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/jsdoc/templates/ptdoc/publish.js ) defines a JavaScript function called publish() that gets invoked by JSDoc. Continuous Integration Accessors Jenkins Build Admin info about our loc

n Accessors Jenkins Build Admin info about our local Jenkins installation On terra.eecs.berkeley.edu we are running a job that checks for updates to the TerraSwarm accessors svn repo every five minutes. If there is a change to the svn repo, then ant

s. If there is a change to the svn repo, then ant is run and JSDoc invoked on the .js files. The output appears at http://terra.eecs.berkeley.edu:8080/job/accessors/JSDoc/ - master copy, but has a :8080 port, which is not accessible everywhere. https

80 port, which is not accessible everywhere. https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/doc/jsdoc/index.html - SCP'd from the master copy by Jenkins. Use this URL as the canonical URL. https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/*PtDoc.xml - SCP'd PtDoc file

rkeley.edu/accessors/*PtDoc.xml - SCP'd PtDoc files generated from the .js files. Importing a .js file imports the corresponding PtDoc.xml file. Problems Input documentation missing in Vergil PTII/bin/vergil $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/demo/Camera

n/vergil $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/demo/Camera/Camera.xml Then click on the Camera Accessor and then click Reload. Then right click on the Camera Accessor and select Documentation -> Get Documentation. The Camera.js accessor file has: * @input t

tion. The Camera.js accessor file has: * @input trigger A trigger input for triggered mode. [$[Get Code]] but the docs output has: <property name="trigger (port-parameter)" class="ptolemy.kernel.util.StringAttribute" value="undefined A trigger input

.StringAttribute" value="undefined A trigger input for triggered mode."> </property> [$[Get Code]] The Input Ports input No port description. [$[Get Code]] Interestingly, the output port doc works. Back to the Software Configuration Management pag
